title: Sarcomphalus mistol
taxonomy: Sarcomphalus mistol belongs to the family Rhamnaceae, commonly known as the buckthorn family of flowering plants. This plant is native to the Americas and is often found in the southwestern regions of the United States and northern Mexico. Sarcomphalus mistol is a deciduous shrub or small tree with small white flowers and edible red fruits. The plant's classification also places it in the genus Sarcomphalus, which is a group of plants closely related to the genus Ziziphus. In terms of broader classification, Sarcomphalus mistol falls within the clade of flowering plants, specifically in the kingdom Plantae.

intro: Sarcomphalus mistol, also known as Mistol, is a deciduous shrub native to the Andean region of South America. The plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, offering several health benefits, including anti-inflammatory and antioxidant effects, as well as the potential to alleviate symptoms of gastrointestinal issues and respiratory problems.
